concepts.title: Adsorption is a surface phenomenon
concepts.description: Adsorption is a surface phenomenon where the molecules of a substance accumulate on the surface of another substance. This is in contrast to absorption, where the substance penetrates the bulk of the other substance.

concepts.title: Types of Adsorption
concepts.description: There are two types of adsorption: Physical Adsorption and Chemical Adsorption. Physical Adsorption is a reversible process and occurs due to van der Waals forces, while Chemical Adsorption is an irreversible process and occurs due to the formation of chemical bonds.

concepts.title: Saha's adsorption isotherm
concepts.description: Saha's adsorption isotherm relates the amount of gas adsorbed to the pressure of the gas at a given temperature. It is given by the equation: x = (aP) / (1 + aP), where x is the amount of gas adsorbed and a is a constant.

concepts.title: Mnemonics for Types of Adsorption
concepts.description: To remember the types of adsorption, use the mnemonic 'P-C' which stands for Physical-Chemical. This will help you associate Physical Adsorption with Physical-Chemical forces and Chemical Adsorption with Chemical bonds.

concepts.title: Confusing Adsorption with Absorption
concepts.description: Many students confuse adsorption with absorption. However, adsorption is a surface phenomenon where the substance accumulates on the surface, while absorption is a bulk phenomenon where the substance penetrates the bulk. Be careful to distinguish between the two.

concepts.title: Units of Adsorption Co-efficient
concepts.description: The adsorption co-efficient (a) is typically expressed in units of ml/g atm or m^2/g. Be familiar with these units to understand the adsorption isotherms and other related equations.

concepts.title: Role of Surface Tension
concepts.description: Surface tension plays a significant role in adsorption. It affects the amount of substance that can be adsorbed on the surface. A higher surface tension increases the adsorption capacity, while a lower surface tension decreases it.

concepts.title: Factors Affecting Adsorption
concepts.description: Adsorption is affected by several factors, including temperature, pressure, surface area, and nature of the adsorbent and adsorbate. Understanding these factors is crucial to predicting the adsorption behavior of a substance.
